sourceMovie.title: Walk of Shame
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Walk of Shame works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Walk of Shame clicked for you, Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Walk of Shame?
faq.answer: Try Home Alone 2: Lost in New York, Pretty Woman, Borat: Cultural Learnings of America for Make Benefit Glorious Nation of Kazakhstan—each shares DNA with Walk of Shame while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Walk of Shame?
faq.answer: Walk of Shame (2014) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Walk of Shame worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
